title: SieveError struct missing #[non_exhaustive]
description: SieveErrorKind has #[non_exhaustive] (parse_error.rs:41) but SieveError does not (parse_error.rs:30-34). SieveError is a public struct with public fields (message: String, kind: SieveErrorKind). Without #[non_exhaustive], downstream crates can struct-initialize SieveError directly and pattern-destructure it, creating a stability commitment that prevents adding fields (e.g. a source span or backtrace) in a semver-compatible release. Idiomatic Rust practice for public error structs intended to evolve is to mark them #[non_exhaustive].
